our_tours.content:
African Citril Safaris is a premier safari company specialising in bespoke wildlife experiences across East Africa. Our expert guides bring decades of field experience, ensuring every journey is safe, immersive, and unforgettable.

We craft each itinerary around your interests — from the Great Migration in the Masai Mara to the pristine wilderness of the Serengeti — combining comfort with authentic adventure.

section_4.title: Additional Services
section_4.additional_services: We offer airport transfers, accommodation bookings, and visa assistance to make your journey seamless from start to finish.

mission_and_values.title: Our Mission & Values
mission_and_values.content: We are committed to delivering extraordinary safari experiences while supporting conservation efforts and local communities across East Africa. Every trip you take with us contributes directly to wildlife protection and the livelihoods of the people who share these landscapes with the animals we treasure.

about.title: About African Citril Safaris
about.about_us:
African Citril Safaris was founded with a singular passion: to share the magic of East Africa's wilderness with the world. Based in Nairobi, Kenya, we are a team of dedicated conservationists, naturalists, and hospitality professionals who believe that travel can be a force for good.

We work with a carefully selected network of lodges, camps, and guides who share our values of sustainability, authenticity, and excellence.

our_story.title: Our Story
our_story.content:
Our journey began over a decade ago when our founder, a lifelong wildlife enthusiast, set out to create a safari company that went beyond the ordinary. Frustrated by one-size-fits-all itineraries, he built African Citril Safaris on the belief that every traveller deserves a personalised experience rooted in genuine connection with nature.

Today, we have guided hundreds of guests from around the world through some of the most spectacular landscapes on earth, earning a reputation for excellence, integrity, and deep knowledge of the bush.

our_values.title: Our Values
our_values.content:
Conservation First — A portion of every booking supports wildlife conservation projects and anti-poaching initiatives in Kenya and Tanzania.

Community Partnership — We work directly with local Maasai and Samburu communities, ensuring tourism benefits the people who are the true custodians of these lands.

Authenticity — We favour small groups, off-the-beaten-path routes, and genuine cultural exchanges over mass-market tourism.

our_mission.title: Our Mission
our_mission.content: To curate transformative safari experiences that connect people with the natural world, foster a lasting commitment to conservation, and create meaningful economic opportunities for East African communities.

our_services.title: Our Services
our_services.content:
Bespoke Safaris — Fully tailored itineraries built around your schedule, interests, and budget.

Group Departures — Scheduled small-group safaris for solo travellers and couples looking to share the experience.

Honeymoon Packages — Romantic itineraries combining bush camps with coastal retreats on the Kenyan or Zanzibar coast.

Photography Safaris — Specialist trips designed for wildlife photographers, with expert guides who know the light and the animals.

Family Safaris — Child-friendly itineraries with experienced guides trained to make the bush accessible and exciting for younger travellers.

destination.name: Kenya
destination.content: Kenya is the birthplace of safari. From the iconic Masai Mara — home of the Great Migration — to the dramatic landscapes of Amboseli beneath Mount Kilimanjaro, Kenya offers an unmatched diversity of wildlife and scenery. Our Kenya itineraries take you beyond the well-trodden paths to hidden conservancies where you may have the bush entirely to yourself.

destination.name: Tanzania
destination.content: Tanzania is home to the Serengeti, Ngorongoro Crater, and the spice island of Zanzibar — a combination of wildlife spectacle and cultural richness that is hard to match anywhere in the world. Whether you are watching lion cubs play on the crater floor or tracing the wildebeest migration across the endless plains, Tanzania never fails to leave a lasting impression.

acf.title: Best of Kenya Safari
acf.description: This flagship Kenya itinerary takes you through the very best the country has to offer. Starting in Nairobi, you will journey north to the Samburu National Reserve before heading south to the world-famous Masai Mara, finishing at the great Rift Valley lakes. Expect extraordinary game viewing at every turn — big cats, elephants, and the endemic species that make Samburu unique.

itinerary.activity: Arrive at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport. You will be met by your African Citril Safaris guide and transferred to your hotel in Nairobi. Enjoy a welcome dinner and briefing for the journey ahead.

Early morning flight or road transfer to Samburu. Afternoon game drive in Samburu National Reserve, home to the Northern Specials — Grevy's zebra, reticulated giraffe, Beisa oryx, Somali ostrich, and gerenuk. Overnight at Samburu camp.

Full day in Samburu with morning and evening game drives. The Ewaso Ng'iro River is a magnet for wildlife — look out for large crocodiles and elephants coming to drink. Optional village visit to a Samburu manyatta in the afternoon.

Transfer south to Lake Nakuru National Park. Famous for its flocks of flamingos and a sanctuary for both black and white rhino, Nakuru offers a very different landscape to Samburu. Afternoon game drive. Overnight at lodge.

Morning game drive at Nakuru then transfer to the Masai Mara. Check in to your tented camp and enjoy an evening game drive across the open savannah. The Mara is home to the highest density of lion in Africa.

Full day in the Masai Mara. Morning and afternoon game drives with a packed lunch enjoyed in the bush. Depending on the season, witness the dramatic river crossings of the Great Migration as millions of wildebeest push north from Tanzania.
Optional early morning hot air balloon safari over the Mara plains, followed by a bush breakfast. Final afternoon game drive before a farewell dinner at camp under the stars.
Morning flight back to Nairobi. Transfer to the airport for your international departure, or extend your stay with a beach add-on on the Kenyan coast.

acf.title: Big Five Safari
acf.description: The ultimate bucket-list safari. This carefully designed itinerary gives you the best possible chance of encountering all five of Africa's most iconic animals — lion, leopard, elephant, buffalo, and rhino — across two of East Africa's finest ecosystems. From the wide-open plains of the Serengeti to the rhino sanctuary at Lake Nakuru, every day brings a new possibility.

itinerary.activity: Arrive in Nairobi and transfer to your hotel. Evening briefing with your guide to outline the adventure ahead. Overnight in Nairobi.

Drive south to Amboseli, set against the iconic backdrop of Mount Kilimanjaro. Amboseli is famous for its large elephant herds and offers outstanding views of Africa's highest peak on clear mornings. Afternoon game drive.
Full day in Amboseli. Morning game drive followed by a visit to a Maasai village. Evening drive as the light turns golden around Kilimanjaro.

Transfer north to Lake Nakuru National Park, a stronghold for both black and white rhino in Kenya. Afternoon game drive targeting the park's resident rhino population as well as lion, leopard, and waterbuck.
Transfer west to the Masai Mara. Check in and evening game drive. The Mara ecosystem supports lions, leopards, elephants, and large buffalo herds — four of the Big Five in a single landscape.
Full day exploring the Mara. Focus game drives on predator activity in the early morning and late afternoon. Sundowner drinks in the bush as the sun drops below the Oloololo Escarpment.
Cross the border into Tanzania and enter the Serengeti. Afternoon game drive through the iconic endless plains. The Serengeti is the stage for the largest animal migration on earth.
Full day in the Serengeti with morning and afternoon game drives. Depending on the season, track the wildebeest herds or look for the resident cheetah coalitions on the open grasslands.

Descend into the Ngorongoro Crater, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of Africa's greatest wildlife spectacles. The crater floor hosts an extraordinary concentration of wildlife including the endangered black rhino, completing the Big Five for most visitors.
Morning transfer to Arusha or Kilimanjaro International Airport for your onward flight. A journey you will never forget.

acf.title: Highlights of Kenya
acf.description: A perfectly paced introduction to Kenya for first-time safari-goers. In six days you will experience the diversity of Kenyan wildlife across three distinct ecosystems — the dry semi-arid north at Samburu, the Rift Valley lakes, and the legendary Masai Mara. Our experienced guides ensure you see and understand as much as possible within a compact timeframe.

itinerary.activity: Arrive in Nairobi and fly or drive to Samburu National Reserve. Check in to camp and enjoy an afternoon game drive along the Ewaso Ng'iro River.
Full day in Samburu with morning and sunset game drives. Look for the Northern Specials — species found nowhere else in Kenya — and the large elephant herds that congregate along the river.
Fly south to Lake Nakuru. Afternoon game drive in the national park, famous for its flamingos, rhino sanctuary, and tree-climbing lions. Overnight at lakeside lodge.
Transfer west to the Masai Mara. Arrive in time for an evening game drive across the open plains. Check in to your tented camp overlooking the savannah.

Full day in the Masai Mara with morning and afternoon game drives. The Mara offers year-round exceptional game viewing — predator sightings, elephant herds, and vast plains teeming with resident wildlife.
Morning game drive then fly back to Nairobi for your international connection. Optional half-day city tour of Nairobi if your flight allows.

acf.title: Best of Tanzania
acf.description: Tanzania is home to some of the world's most spectacular wildlife areas, and this itinerary takes you to all of them. From the vast grasslands of the Serengeti to the wildlife-packed Ngorongoro Crater and the remote Tarangire — famous for its enormous elephant herds and ancient baobab trees — this is Tanzania at its finest, ending with a few days of relaxation on the idyllic island of Zanzibar.

itinerary.activity: Arrive at Kilimanjaro International Airport. Transfer to your hotel in Arusha for an overnight stay. Evening briefing with your guide.

Drive to Tarangire National Park, home to the largest elephant population in northern Tanzania. The ancient baobab trees provide a dramatic backdrop for afternoon game drives along the Tarangire River.
Full day in Tarangire. Look for large predators — lion prides are frequently seen here — as well as the park's impressive variety of dry-country antelope and hundreds of bird species.
Transfer to the Serengeti via the Ngorongoro highlands. Enter the Serengeti and enjoy your first game drive across the iconic plains. Check in to your central Serengeti camp.
Full day in the Serengeti. Spend the morning tracking predators — cheetah, leopard, and lion are all regularly sighted — and the afternoon following large herds of zebra and wildebeest.
A second full day to explore the Serengeti's diverse habitats — from the kopjes of the central Seronera to the acacia woodlands of the western corridor. Bush lunch in a spectacular setting.
Transfer to the Ngorongoro Conservation Area. Check in to your crater rim lodge with sweeping views into the caldera. Afternoon descent into the crater for a game drive on the crater floor.
Morning flight from Arusha or Kilimanjaro to Zanzibar. Transfer to your beach hotel on the east coast. Afternoon at leisure — swim, snorkel, or simply unwind after the safari.
Final morning on the beach before transfer to Zanzibar airport for your onward or homebound flight. A perfect ending to an extraordinary East African journey.
